content.verdict: Rixos Premium Dubrovnik is a large, full-service resort on the seafront near Dubrovnik that offers exceptional value for its five-star rating, particularly if you choose the all-inclusive option. The dramatic cave bar, multiple pools, and large spa make it genuinely fun and hedonistic; however, its scale and family-friendly orientation mean it's better for couples who enjoy a lively resort atmosphere than those seeking an intimate romantic retreat. Think of it as a well-priced gateway to Dubrovnik with great facilities rather than an exclusively romantic hideaway.
content.best_room: Book a Prestige Sea View Room or a Junior Suite with a sea-facing balcony on the upper floors — you want to be above the pool terraces for genuine privacy and the best views over the Adriatic and Lokrum Island. The premium rooms in the newly renovated wing have a cleaner, more contemporary feel that suits a honeymoon better than the older standard rooms.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Cave Bar First Night
itinerary_7_nights.description: Check in and explore the resort's dramatic seafront position, then head to the famous cave bar built into the coastal rock for welcome cocktails — the combination of natural stone, cocktails, and the sound of the Adriatic sets an exhilarating mood. Dinner at the resort's main restaurant with the all-inclusive option lets you relax completely on your first night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach & Pool Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Stake out the best loungers on the private beach early morning, then move between the beach and the resort's terraced pools as the day heats up. The Rixos spa offers a full menu of treatments — book a couples' hammam session, which is a highlight of the Turkish-influenced spa programme. A long lunch at the beach restaurant completes a perfectly relaxed day.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dubrovnik Old Town
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take a taxi into Dubrovnik's Old Town (about 4km) and spend the day exploring the ancient walled city. Walk the city walls in the morning for the best light and fewer crowds, visit the Cathedral Treasury, and find a small konoba in a side street for lunch. In the evening, watch the sunset from the Buža bar clinging to the city walls before returning to the resort.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Water Sports & Adriatic Adventure
itinerary_7_nights.description: The resort's water sports centre offers jet skis, paddleboards, kayaks, and speedboat excursions — perfect for an active honeymoon day. Book a speedboat tour to the Elafiti Islands or the Blue Cave near Korčula for a day of swimming in impossibly turquoise water. Return in the late afternoon for a couples' massage at the spa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Wellness Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Dedicate a full day to the spa — the Rixos spa is one of the largest in the Dubrovnik area, with a full hammam, plunge pools, steam rooms, and a comprehensive treatment menu. Book a full-day couples' spa package, have lunch at the spa café, and finish with an afternoon lounging in the thermal area. Evening drinks at the cave bar feel especially earned.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Korcula Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take a ferry or organised excursion to Korčula island, birthplace of Marco Polo, with its beautifully preserved medieval walled town. Explore the narrow herringbone streets, visit the Cathedral of St Mark, and have a long seafood lunch on the harbour before catching the afternoon ferry back. Stop at the cave bar on your return for a nightcap.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last morning: an early swim in the sea, breakfast on the terrace, and a final wander around the resort's cliff path with views back to the Old Town glowing in the morning sun. The all-inclusive option means you can eat and drink to your heart's content right up until checkout.

caveats: Rixos is a large resort with a kids club and family facilities — during July and August it becomes very busy with families, significantly diluting the romantic atmosphere for honeymooners.

The all-inclusive model, while good value, can create a somewhat insular holiday-camp atmosphere; couples who want to explore authentic Croatian dining and nightlife may find themselves over-relying on resort facilities.
The location, while on the coast, requires a taxi to reach Dubrovnik's Old Town — it's not within easy walking distance of the historic centre.

faqs.question: Is Rixos Premium Dubrovnik good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: It can be a very enjoyable honeymoon base, particularly for couples who love resort-style holidays with pools, spas, and everything included. The five-star facilities and dramatic cliff location are genuinely impressive. However, the large scale and family presence mean it isn't the most intimate or exclusively romantic option in the Dubrovnik area — couples seeking a quiet, adults-only environment should look elsewhere.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: June and the first half of September are ideal — warm enough for beach and water sports, quieter than July and August, and the sea is still at its warmest. July and August see the resort at full capacity with families; while facilities are excellent, it's harder to find a quiet corner or a sun lounger by the pool without staking your claim early.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: If choosing all-inclusive, confirm the package details and any premium dining upgrades before arrival. Book spa treatments in advance, especially for weekends or peak summer. If you want to take day trips to Korčula or the Elafiti Islands, book these through the concierge before arrival as popular excursions fill quickly in summer.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, Rixos Premium Dubrovnik is not adults-only. The resort actively caters to families with a dedicated kids club, children's pool, and family rooms. If you are honeymooning in peak summer and adults-only is important to you, this is the wrong hotel.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: A Prestige Sea View Room or Junior Suite with sea-facing balcony on the upper floors of the main building is the best choice. These rooms offer direct sea views over the Adriatic, a private balcony, and enough space to feel genuinely comfortable. Avoid garden-facing or pool-side rooms if you want sea views and privacy.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Dubrovnik Airport (DBV) and take a taxi or private transfer — the hotel is approximately 8km from the airport, a journey of around 15–20 minutes. The resort is on the Lapad peninsula, west of the Old Town; taxis to Old Town take around 10 minutes. The hotel can arrange airport transfers on request.
